question.text: The Scheme Information Document (SID) of a mutual fund contains:

answer.text: Details of the particular scheme being offered
Statutory information about the mutual fund or AMC
Only the key information an investor needs before investing
The audited financial statements of the scheme

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Mutual fund scheme-related documents consist of three parts: (a) Statement of Additional Information (SAI) — statutory information about the mutual fund or AMC, (b) Scheme Information Document (SID) — details of the particular scheme, and (c) Key Information Memorandum (KIM) — key information an investor must know before investing. These are available free of cost at ISCs or AMC offices.
**The Trap:** SAI (not SID) contains statutory information about the AMC. KIM (not SID) is the summary document with key information. SID is the detailed scheme-specific document. Each document serves a distinct purpose.
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. Understanding the different scheme documents and their contents is frequently tested in all MF exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always provide the KIM to investors before they invest — it is the most accessible summary document. For detailed analysis, refer to the SID. These documents must be read before recommending any scheme.
